name: Chana Masala
tagline: Punjabi chickpea curry — fiber, plant protein, slow-burn carbs
highlight: Black tea bag during pressure cook gives the dark, restaurant-style colour

hero.alt: Dark spiced chickpeas with onion-tomato gravy, ginger juliennes on top
hero.caption: chana masala — dark, tangy, ginger-finished

healthFacts: Chickpeas deliver ~15g protein and 13g fiber per cooked cup — strongest plant duo.
Soluble fiber (galactans) lowers LDL cholesterol; resistant starch feeds gut bacteria.
Glycemic index ~28 — among the lowest of all carb sources.
Iron, folate, manganese, and copper all hit double-digit DV per serving.

steps.title: Pressure-cook the chana
steps.detail: Pressure cook the soaked chickpeas with 4 cups water, salt, and a black tea bag (tied in muslin) for 6–7 whistles. Discard the tea bag — it's done its colour job.
steps.tip: Skip the tea bag and the gravy stays beige; it's the secret behind the deep black colour.

steps.title: Make the masala
steps.detail: Heat oil, crackle cumin. Sauté onion till deep brown, add ginger-garlic, then tomato purée. Cook till oil rises (8 min). Add chana masala and cook 1 minute.
steps.tip: Brown the onion well — it builds the dish's umami depth.

steps.title: Combine and simmer
steps.detail: Tip in the cooked chickpeas with their cooking liquid. Simmer 15 minutes. Mash a few chickpeas against the side to thicken the gravy. Finish with lemon and ginger juliennes.
steps.tip: Serve with bhature for the indulgent version, or with brown rice for the daily version.
